text: The first-use flow should include clear onboarding, setup guidance, and privacy controls.
A clear onboarding checklist with setup guidance would make the first session easier.
Users need onboarding guidance, examples, and visible privacy controls before setup.
A setup checklist, short examples, and privacy settings would improve the first experience.
The product should make onboarding clear and show privacy controls during setup.
Guided setup with examples and visible privacy choices would reduce first-use uncertainty.
Trust would increase with transparent evidence, clear privacy notes, and audit logs.
Users need evidence summaries, privacy information, and an audit trail for recommendations.
A product feels trustworthy when evidence, privacy controls, and logs are easy to inspect.
Clear evidence links and privacy documentation would support trust in the recommendation.
Trust depends on privacy controls, traceable recommendations, and concise evidence notes.
The system should show evidence, privacy settings, and audit information in one place.
The team should address integration effort, pricing clarity, and security documentation.
Adoption depends on security notes, integration planning, and transparent pricing.
Before adoption, users need clearer security documentation and integration guidance.
The main concern is whether pricing, integrations, and security requirements are predictable.
Teams will ask for pricing clarity, security review material, and integration examples.
The product should document security, integration effort, and pricing before rollout.
